# The 3-touch champagne sequence

Plain text, one inbox, 1:1. The first touch is generated per-target by the script
(`queue.md`). Touches 2 and 3 are below. Space them ~3 and ~7 days after touch 1.
Send only to people who did not reply. Stop the moment someone says no.

Placeholders: `{firstName}`, `{company}`, `{signalPlain}`, `{landingUrl}`.

---

## Touch 1 — the brief (auto-generated)

The script writes this one. It names their real, public moment, explains that we
found them the way the product finds leads, and links the matching landing page.
This is the demo.

---

## Touch 2 — the one-line proof (day ~3)

> Subject: re: {company}
>
> Hi {firstName},
>
> Following up with the part that matters: the brief I would build on {company}
> is not a guess. Every line links to the public thing it came from, so your rep
> never sounds like they are fishing.
>
> Three of those, free, no card: {landingUrl}
>
> — Lilly, Buying Moment

---

## Touch 3 — the break-up (day ~7)

> Subject: closing the loop, {firstName}
>
> Hi {firstName},
>
> Last note from me. If catching companies the moment they are ready is not a
> priority right now, no worries at all, I will get out of your inbox.
>
> If it is ever useful, your first three briefs are here whenever you want them,
> no card: {landingUrl}
>
> Either way, good luck with {company}.
>
> — Lilly, Buying Moment

---

## Notes

- Most replies land on touch 2 or 3. The break-up especially. Do not skip them.
- Keep every touch short. The link does the selling; the email earns the click.
- If someone replies "in," send them straight to the same `{landingUrl}` and let
  the reverse trial do the rest. No demo call needed unless they ask.
